_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in collecting relevant data from a variety of sources
reference_document_element_description: Skill in translating a business question into a data query, identifying the correct data sources, joins, filters, and aggregations required to answer the question accurately.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI AB domain. Skill AB-S-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-008 (DG, cosine: 0.527), AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.522).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.8/10 (raw 0.679). Shared vocabulary: [data, skill, sources]. NICE-only terms [collecting, relevant, variety] vs WIDAI-only terms [accurately, aggregations, answer, business, correct, filters] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.887 (P=0.905, R=0.870). P exceeds R by 0.035 — the NICE element's 10 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Skill in collecting relevant data from a variety of sources" and "Skill in translating a business question into a data query, identifying the corr" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.553 < STS 0.679 (gap -0.126). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [collecting, relevant, variety] and [accurately, aggregations, answer] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 19.2%. Shared tokens: [data, skill, sources]. NICE-only: [collecting, relevant, variety]. WIDAI-only: [accurately, aggregations, answer, business, correct, filters].
